text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any developer. The main goal is to make your code readable and easy to understand, not just for the computer, but for other people—including your future self. A good starting point is to follow clear naming conventions. Variable and function names should describe what they do. For example, use `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. Keep your functions short; each function should do only one thing. This makes your code easier to test and reuse. Ultimately, clean code improves maintainability, saving time and reducing errors when you need to update or fix it later.
